1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     5→PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
     6→CLUSTER_NAME="micro-commerce"
     7→NAMESPACE="micro-commerce"
     8→SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION="v0.27.3"
     9→ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
    10→
    11→# Color output
    12→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    13→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    14→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    15→
    16→info() { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    17→warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    18→
    19→# --- Step 1: Create kind cluster ---
    20→if kind get clusters 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${CLUSTER_NAME}$"; then
    21→  info "Cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}' already exists, skipping creation."
    22→else
    23→  info "Creating kind cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}'..."
    24→  kind create cluster --name "$CLUSTER_NAME" --config "$SCRIPT_DIR/kind-config.yaml"
    25→fi
    26→
    27→# --- Step 2: Set kubectl context ---
    28→info "Setting kubectl context..."
    29→kubectl cluster-info --context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1
    30→kubectl config use-context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    31→
    32→# --- Step 3: Install SealedSecrets controller ---
    33→info "Installing SealedSecrets controller ${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}..."
    34→kubectl apply -f "https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets/releases/download/${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}/controller.yaml"
    35→
    36→# --- Step 4: Wait for SealedSecrets controller ---
    37→info "Waiting for SealedSecrets controller to be ready..."
    38→kubectl rollout status deployment/sealed-secrets-controller -n kube-system --timeout=120s
    39→
    40→# --- Step 5: Create namespace ---
    41→info "Creating namespace '${NAMESPACE}'..."
    42→kubectl create namespace "$NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    43→
    44→# --- Step 6: Seal dev secrets and apply directly ---
    45→info "Sealing dev secrets..."
    46→
    47→seal_secret() {
    48→  local name="$1"
    49→  local output="$2"
    50→  shift 2
    51→  # Remaining args are --from-literal pairs
    52→
    53→  kubectl create secret generic "$name" \
    54→    --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
    55→    "$@" \
    56→    --dry-run=client -o yaml | \
    57→  kubeseal \
    58→    --controller-name=sealed-secrets-controller \
    59→    --controller-namespace=kube-system \
    60→    --format yaml > "$output"
    61→
    62→  info "  Sealed: $name -> $output"
    63→}
    64→
    65→seal_secret "postgres-credentials" \
    66→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    67→  --from-literal=username=postgres \
    68→  --from-literal=password=postgres
    69→
    70→seal_secret "rabbitmq-credentials" \
    71→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    72→  --from-literal=username=guest \
    73→  --from-literal=password=guest
    74→
    75→seal_secret "keycloak-credentials" \
    76→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    77→  --from-literal=admin-username=admin \
    78→  --from-literal=admin-password=admin
    79→
    80→info "Applying sealed secrets..."
    81→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml"
    82→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml"
    83→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml"
    84→
    85→# --- Step 7: Install ArgoCD ---
    86→info "Installing ArgoCD ${ARGOCD_VERSION}..."
    87→kubectl create namespace argocd --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    88→kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
    89→  -f "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/${ARGOCD_VERSION}/manifests/install.yaml"
    90→
    91→# --- Step 8: Wait for ArgoCD to be ready ---
    92→info "Waiting for ArgoCD to be ready..."
    93→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
    94→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-repo-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
    95→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-applicationset-controller -n argocd --timeout=120s
    96→
    97→# --- Step 9: Configure ArgoCD for local access ---
    98→info "Configuring ArgoCD for local access..."
    99→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-cmd-params-cm.yaml"
   100→kubectl rollout restart deployment argocd-server -n argocd
   101→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   102→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-server-nodeport.yaml"
   103→
   104→# --- Step 10: Build application container images ---
   105→info "Building application container images..."
   106→
   107→info "  Building ApiService..."
   108→dotnet publish "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.ApiService/MicroCommerce.ApiService.csproj" \
   109→  /t:PublishContainer \
   110→  -p:ContainerRegistry="" \
   111→  -p:ContainerRepository=apiservice \
   112→  -p:ContainerImageTag=dev \
   113→  --nologo -v quiet
   114→
   115→info "  Building Gateway..."
   116→dotnet publish "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Gateway/MicroCommerce.Gateway.csproj" \
   117→  /t:PublishContainer \
   118→  -p:ContainerRegistry="" \
   119→  -p:ContainerRepository=gateway \
   120→  -p:ContainerImageTag=dev \
   121→  --nologo -v quiet
   122→
   123→info "  Building Web..."
   124→docker build \
   125→  -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Web/Dockerfile" \
   126→  -t web:dev \
   127→  "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Web" \
   128→  --quiet
   129→
   130→# --- Step 11: Load images into kind ---
   131→info "Loading images into kind cluster..."
   132→kind load docker-image apiservice:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   133→kind load docker-image gateway:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   134→kind load docker-image web:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   135→
   136→# --- Step 12: Apply ArgoCD root app-of-apps ---
   137→info "Applying ArgoCD root app-of-apps..."
   138→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/root-app.yaml"
   139→
   140→# --- Step 13: Wait for ArgoCD to sync all applications ---
   141→info "Waiting for ArgoCD to sync all applications..."
   142→sleep 10
   143→
   144→info "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
   145→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=postgres -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   146→
   147→info "Waiting for RabbitMQ..."
   148→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=rabbitmq -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   149→
   150→info "Waiting for Keycloak (may take up to 3 minutes for realm import)..."
   151→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=keycloak -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   152→
   153→info "Waiting for ApiService (may take up to 3 minutes for first-boot migrations)..."
   154→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=apiservice -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   155→
   156→info "Waiting for Gateway..."
   157→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=gateway -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   158→
   159→info "Waiting for Web..."
   160→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=web -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   161→
   162→info "Waiting for OTEL Collector..."
   163→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=otel-collector -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   164→
   165→info "Waiting for Aspire Dashboard..."
   166→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=aspire-dashboard -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   167→
   168→# --- Step 14: Print access info ---
   169→ARGOCD_PASSWORD=$(kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret \
   170→  -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 --decode)
   171→
   172→echo ""
   173→info "Full stack ready!"
   174→echo ""
   175→echo "Access points:"
   176→echo "  PostgreSQL:          localhost:35432 (user: postgres, pass: postgres, db: appdb)"
   177→echo "  RabbitMQ Management: http://localhost:35672 (user: guest, pass: guest)"
   178→echo "  Keycloak Admin:      http://localhost:38080 (user: admin, pass: admin)"
   179→echo "  Gateway (API):       http://localhost:38800"
   180→echo "  ArgoCD UI:           http://localhost:38443 (user: admin, pass: ${ARGOCD_PASSWORD})"
   181→echo "  Aspire Dashboard:    http://localhost:38888"
   182→echo ""
   183→echo "Storefront: http://localhost:38800 (via Gateway)"
   184→echo ""
   185→echo "Useful commands:"
   186→echo "  kubectl get pods -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   187→echo "  kubectl get applications -n argocd"
   188→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/apiservice -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   189→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/gateway -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   190→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/web -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   191→echo "  kind delete cluster --name ${CLUSTER_NAME}"
   192→

1→apiVersion: apps/v1
     2→kind: Deployment
     3→metadata:
     4→  name: keycloak
     5→  labels:
     6→    app: keycloak
     7→    app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak
     8→    app.kubernetes.io/component: auth
     9→    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
    10→spec:
    11→  replicas: 1
    12→  selector:
    13→    matchLabels:
    14→      app: keycloak
    15→      app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak
    16→      app.kubernetes.io/component: auth
    17→      app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
    18→  template:
    19→    metadata:
    20→      labels:
    21→        app: keycloak
    22→        app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak
    23→        app.kubernetes.io/component: auth
    24→        app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
    25→    spec:
    26→      serviceAccountName: keycloak
    27→      containers:
    28→      - name: keycloak
    29→        securityContext:
    30→          runAsNonRoot: true
    31→          readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
    32→          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
    33→          capabilities:
    34→            drop:
    35→              - ALL
    36→        image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.0
    37→        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    38→        args: ["start-dev", "--import-realm"]
    39→        env:
    40→        - name: KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME
    41→          valueFrom:
    42→            secretKeyRef:
    43→              name: keycloak-credentials
    44→              key: admin-username
    45→        - name: KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD
    46→          valueFrom:
    47→            secretKeyRef:
    48→              name: keycloak-credentials
    49→              key: admin-password
    50→        - name: KC_HEALTH_ENABLED
    51→          value: "true"
    52→        ports:
    53→        - containerPort: 8080
    54→          name: http
    55→        - containerPort: 9000
    56→          name: management
    57→        volumeMounts:
    58→        - name: realm-config
    59→          mountPath: /opt/keycloak/data/import
    60→        startupProbe:
    61→          httpGet:
    62→            path: /health/started
    63→            port: 9000
    64→          initialDelaySeconds: 15
    65→          periodSeconds: 3
    66→          failureThreshold: 20
    67→        livenessProbe:
    68→          httpGet:
    69→            path: /health/live
    70→            port: 9000
    71→          periodSeconds: 15
    72→        readinessProbe:
    73→          httpGet:
    74→            path: /health/ready
    75→            port: 9000
    76→          periodSeconds: 10
    77→        resources:
    78→          requests:
    79→            memory: 512Mi
    80→            cpu: 500m
    81→          limits:
    82→            memory: 1Gi
    83→            cpu: "1"
    84→      volumes:
    85→      - name: realm-config
    86→        configMap:
    87→          name: keycloak-realm
    88→

   131→
   132→<frontmatter_guidance>
   133→**Purpose:** Enable automatic context assembly via dependency graph. Frontmatter makes summary metadata machine-readable so plan-phase can scan all summaries quickly and select relevant ones based on dependencies.
   134→
   135→**Fast scanning:** Frontmatter is first ~25 lines, cheap to scan across all summaries without reading full content.
   136→
   137→**Dependency graph:** `requires`/`provides`/`affects` create explicit links between phases, enabling transitive closure for context selection.
   138→
   139→**Subsystem:** Primary categorization (auth, payments, ui, api, database, infra, testing) for detecting related phases.
   140→
   141→**Tags:** Searchable technical keywords (libraries, frameworks, tools) for tech stack awareness.
   142→
   143→**Key-files:** Important files for @context references in PLAN.md.
   144→
   145→**Patterns:** Established conventions future phases should maintain.
   146→
   147→**Population:** Frontmatter is populated during summary creation in execute-plan.md. See `<step name="create_summary">` for field-by-field guidance.
   148→</frontmatter_guidance>
   149→
   150→<one_liner_rules>
   151→The one-liner MUST be substantive:
   152→
   153→**Good:**
   154→- "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library"
   155→- "Prisma schema with User, Session, and Product models"
   156→- "Dashboard with real-time metrics via Server-Sent Events"
   157→
   158→**Bad:**
   159→- "Phase complete"
   160→- "Authentication implemented"
   161→- "Foundation finished"
   162→- "All tasks done"
   163→
   164→The one-liner should tell someone what actually shipped.
   165→</one_liner_rules>
   166→
   167→<example>
   168→```markdown
   169→# Phase 1: Foundation Summary
   170→
   171→**JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library, Prisma User model, and protected API middleware**
   172→
   173→## Performance
   174→
   175→- **Duration:** 28 min
   176→- **Started:** 2025-01-15T14:22:10Z
   177→- **Completed:** 2025-01-15T14:50:33Z
   178→- **Tasks:** 5
   179→- **Files modified:** 8
   180→
   181→## Accomplishments
   182→- User model with email/password auth
   183→- Login/logout endpoints with httpOnly JWT cookies
   184→- Protected route middleware checking token validity
   185→- Refresh token rotation on each request
   186→
   187→## Files Created/Modified
   188→- `prisma/schema.prisma` - User and Session models
   189→- `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts` - Login endpoint
   190→- `src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts` - Logout endpoint
   191→- `src/middleware.ts` - Protected route checks
   192→- `src/lib/auth.ts` - JWT helpers using jose
   193→
   194→## Decisions Made
   195→- Used jose instead of jsonwebtoken (ESM-native, Edge-compatible)
   196→- 15-min access tokens with 7-day refresh tokens
   197→- Storing refresh tokens in database for revocation capability
   198→
   199→## Deviations from Plan
   200→
   201→### Auto-fixed Issues
   202→
   203→**1. [Rule 2 - Missing Critical] Added password hashing with bcrypt**
   204→- **Found during:** Task 2 (Login endpoint implementation)
   205→- **Issue:** Plan didn't specify password hashing - storing plaintext would be critical security flaw
   206→- **Fix:** Added bcrypt hashing on registration, comparison on login with salt rounds 10
   207→- **Files modified:** src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts, src/lib/auth.ts
   208→- **Verification:** Password hash test passes, plaintext never stored
   209→- **Committed in:** abc123f (Task 2 commit)
   210→
   211→**2. [Rule 3 - Blocking] Installed missing jose dependency**
   212→- **Found during:** Task 4 (JWT token generation)
   213→- **Issue:** jose package not in package.json, import failing
   214→- **Fix:** Ran `npm install jose`
   215→- **Files modified:** package.json, package-lock.json
   216→- **Verification:** Import succeeds, build passes
   217→- **Committed in:** def456g (Task 4 commit)
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→**Total deviations:** 2 auto-fixed (1 missing critical, 1 blocking)
   222→**Impact on plan:** Both auto-fixes essential for security and functionality. No scope creep.
   223→
   224→## Issues Encountered
   225→- jsonwebtoken CommonJS import failed in Edge runtime - switched to jose (planned library change, worked as expected)
   226→
   227→## Next Phase Readiness
   228→- Auth foundation complete, ready for feature development
   229→- User registration endpoint needed before public launch
   230→
   231→---
   232→*Phase: 01-foundation*
   233→*Completed: 2025-01-15*
   234→```
   235→</example>
   236→
   237→<guidelines>
   238→**Frontmatter:** MANDATORY - complete all fields. Enables automatic context assembly for future planning.
   239→
   240→**One-liner:** Must be substantive. "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library" not "Authentication implemented".
   241→
   242→**Decisions section:**
   243→- Key decisions made during execution with rationale
   244→- Extracted to STATE.md accumulated context
   245→- Use "None - followed plan as specified" if no deviations
   246→
   247→**After creation:** STATE.md updated with position, decisions, issues.
   248→</guidelines>

# Phase 33 Plan 02: Web Secrets & Keycloak Production Mode Summary

**Web frontend secrets moved to SealedSecret references, Keycloak base switched to production start with dev overlay patch**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 1 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-08T15:13:29Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-08T15:15:10Z
- **Tasks:** 2
- **Files modified:** 6

## Accomplishments
- Removed plain-text AUTH_SECRET and KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET from web deployment manifest
- Web deployment now references web-secrets Secret via secretKeyRef
- Bootstrap script seals and applies web-secrets alongside existing infrastructure secrets
- Keycloak base manifest uses production-mode `start` command
- Dev overlay patches keycloak back to `start-dev` for local development

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Move web secrets to SealedSecret references and update bootstrap** - `9e333e59` (feat)
2. **Task 2: Switch Keycloak base to production mode with dev overlay patch** - `cc1915d5` (feat)

## Files Created/Modified
- `infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml` - AUTH_SECRET and KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET now use secretKeyRef to web-secrets
- `infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh` - Added seal_secret and kubectl apply for web-secrets
- `infra/k8s/base/keycloak/deployment.yaml` - Changed args from start-dev to start
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/keycloak/keycloak-start-dev-patch.yaml` - Strategic merge patch switching to start-dev
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/keycloak/kustomization.yaml` - Dev overlay kustomization referencing base and patch
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml` - Changed keycloak reference from base to overlay directory

## Decisions Made
- Web secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) use secretKeyRef to web-secrets SealedSecret, consistent with infra secret pattern
- Keycloak base uses `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev` via strategic merge patch, matching existing overlay patterns for apiservice/gateway/web

## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.

## Issues Encountered
None

## User Setup Required
None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- All secrets now managed via SealedSecrets (both infrastructure and application)
- Keycloak production/dev mode separation ready for environment-specific deployments
- Ready for next phase in v3.1 hardening milestone

---

*Completed: 2026-03-08*

  </files>
  <action>
For each of the 8 workloads (apiservice, gateway, web, keycloak, postgres, rabbitmq, otel-collector, aspire-dashboard):

1. **Create a ServiceAccount YAML** (`serviceaccount.yaml`) in the workload's base directory:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: {workload-name}
  labels:
    app: {workload-name}
    app.kubernetes.io/name: {workload-name}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: {component}
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
automountServiceAccountToken: false
```

Use the existing `app.kubernetes.io/component` value from each deployment (api, gateway, frontend, auth, database, messaging, observability).

2. **Update each deployment/statefulset** to add:
   - `spec.template.spec.serviceAccountName: {workload-name}` — reference the dedicated ServiceAccount
   - Container-level `securityContext` block on the container:

```yaml
securityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL
```

**Important exceptions for `readOnlyRootFilesystem`:**
- **postgres**: Set `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` — PostgreSQL needs to write to its data directory and /tmp. It already has a PVC mount but also writes to paths outside the mount.
- **keycloak**: Set `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` — Keycloak writes theme caches, tmp files, and data to multiple directories.
- **rabbitmq**: Set `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` — RabbitMQ writes to /var/lib/rabbitmq for mnesia database and other runtime state.

For these three, keep `runAsNonRoot: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, and `capabilities.drop: [ALL]`.

**Also for `runAsNonRoot`:**
- **postgres**: The official postgres image runs as root by default. Add `runAsNonRoot: false` for postgres (it handles privilege dropping internally via `gosu`). Set `runAsUser: 999` (the postgres user) instead — or simply set `runAsNonRoot: false` since the official image manages this internally. Use `runAsNonRoot: false` for postgres to avoid startup failures.

Place the `securityContext` at the container level (under `spec.template.spec.containers[0].securityContext`), NOT at the pod level.

3. **Update each kustomization.yaml** to add `serviceaccount.yaml` to the `resources` list. Keep existing resources in order, add serviceaccount.yaml after the existing entries (deployment.yaml, service.yaml, etc.).

All 8 workloads to process:
- apiservice (component: api)
- gateway (component: gateway)
- web (component: frontend)
- keycloak (component: auth)
- postgres (component: database) — StatefulSet, readOnlyRootFilesystem: false, runAsNonRoot: false
- rabbitmq (component: messaging) — readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
- otel-collector (component: observability)
- aspire-dashboard (component: observability)
  </action>

  <files>

  </files>
  <action>
1. **Update `infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml`** — Replace the two plain-text env values with secretKeyRef:

Change `AUTH_SECRET` from:
```yaml
- name: AUTH_SECRET
  value: "k8s-dev-auth-secret-change-in-production"
```
To:
```yaml
- name: AUTH_SECRET
  valueFrom:
    secretKeyRef:
      name: web-secrets
      key: auth-secret
```

Change `KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET` from:
```yaml
- name: KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET
  value: "nextjs-app-secret"
```
To:
```yaml
- name: KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET
  valueFrom:
    secretKeyRef:
      name: web-secrets
      key: keycloak-client-secret
```

Keep all other env vars unchanged.

2. **Update `infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh`** — Add a `seal_secret` call for `web-secrets` after the existing keycloak-credentials seal (around line 78). Add:

```bash
seal_secret "web-secrets" \
  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/web/sealed-secret.yaml" \
  --from-literal=auth-secret=k8s-dev-auth-secret-change-in-production \
  --from-literal=keycloak-client-secret=nextjs-app-secret
```

Also add the apply command after the existing sealed secret applies (around line 83):
```bash
kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/web/sealed-secret.yaml"
```

The sealed-secret.yaml file will be generated at bootstrap time by kubeseal (not committed to git — it's cluster-specific). The SealedSecret controller will create the actual Secret object that the deployment references.
  </action>

```yaml

```

This makes the base manifest production-ready. The `start` command requires HTTPS configuration in production but the dev overlay will patch back to `start-dev`.

2. **Create `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/keycloak/keycloak-start-dev-patch.yaml`** — A strategic merge patch:

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1

```

```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

resources:
- ../../../base/keycloak

patches:
- path: keycloak-start-dev-patch.yaml
```

4. **Update `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml`** — Change the keycloak resource reference from `../../base/keycloak` to `keycloak/` (the new overlay subdirectory). The resources list should become:

```yaml
- keycloak/
- ../../base/otel-collector
- ../../base/aspire-dashboard
- apiservice/
- gateway/
- web/
```

This mirrors the pattern already used for apiservice/, gateway/, and web/ overlays.

# Phase 33 Plan 01: K8s Security Hardening Summary

**Security contexts and dedicated service accounts added to all 8 K8s workloads with least-privilege defaults and capability dropping**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 1 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-08T15:10:35Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-08T15:11:48Z
- **Tasks:** 1
- **Files modified:** 24

## Accomplishments
- All 8 workloads hardened with container-level securityContext (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, drop ALL capabilities)
- 8 dedicated ServiceAccounts created with automountServiceAccountToken: false
- Appropriate exceptions applied for postgres (runAsNonRoot: false, readOnlyRootFilesystem: false), keycloak (readOnlyRootFilesystem: false), and rabbitmq (readOnlyRootFilesystem: false)
- Kustomize build verified passing with all changes

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Add securityContext to all 8 workloads and create dedicated ServiceAccounts** - `37f52d5f` (feat)

## Files Created/Modified
- `infra/k8s/base/*/serviceaccount.yaml` - Dedicated ServiceAccount per workload with automountServiceAccountToken: false
- `infra/k8s/base/*/deployment.yaml` - Added serviceAccountName and container-level securityContext
- `infra/k8s/base/postgres/statefulset.yaml` - Added serviceAccountName and securityContext (non-root exception)
- `infra/k8s/base/*/kustomization.yaml` - Added serviceaccount.yaml to resources list

## Decisions Made
- postgres uses runAsNonRoot: false because the official postgres image manages privilege dropping internally via gosu
- postgres, keycloak, and rabbitmq use readOnlyRootFilesystem: false due to runtime write requirements (data directories, theme caches, mnesia database)
- securityContext placed at container level (not pod level) for granular per-container control

## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.

## Issues Encountered
None

## User Setup Required
None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- All workloads now have dedicated ServiceAccounts, ready for RBAC policies if needed
- Security contexts established, ready for NetworkPolicy hardening in subsequent phases

**Phase description:**
### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events

**Project instructions:** Read ./CLAUDE.md if exists — follow project-specific guidelines
**Project skills:** Check .claude/skills/ or .agents/skills/ directory (if either exists) — read SKILL.md files, plans should account for project skill rules

    84→### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes

    91→  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing

    93→  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
    94→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete

    97→
    98→### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
    99→**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 30
   101→**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
   104→  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
   105→  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
   106→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
   107→Plans:
   108→- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows
   109→
   110→### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
   111→**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
   112→**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
   113→**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
   114→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   115→  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
   116→  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
   117→  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
   118→  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
   119→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   120→Plans:
   121→- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
   122→- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads
   123→
   124→### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
   125→**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
   126→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
   127→**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04

   129→  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
   130→  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
   131→  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
   132→  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
   133→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   134→Plans:
   135→- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
   136→- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay
   137→
   138→### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
   139→**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
   140→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
   141→**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
   142→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   143→  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
   144→  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
   145→  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
   146→  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
   147→  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
   148→  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
   149→  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
   150→**Plans**: TBD
   151→
   152→### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
   153→**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
   154→**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
   155→**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
   156→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   157→  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
   158→  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
   159→  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
   160→  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
   161→  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
   162→  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces

# Phase 33 Plan 01: K8s Security Hardening Summary

**Security contexts and dedicated service accounts added to all 8 K8s workloads with least-privilege defaults and capability dropping**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 1 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-08T15:10:35Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-08T15:11:48Z
- **Tasks:** 1
- **Files modified:** 24

## Accomplishments
- All 8 workloads hardened with container-level securityContext (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, drop ALL capabilities)
- 8 dedicated ServiceAccounts created with automountServiceAccountToken: false
- Appropriate exceptions applied for postgres (runAsNonRoot: false, readOnlyRootFilesystem: false), keycloak (readOnlyRootFilesystem: false), and rabbitmq (readOnlyRootFilesystem: false)
- Kustomize build verified passing with all changes

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Add securityContext to all 8 workloads and create dedicated ServiceAccounts** - `37f52d5f` (feat)

## Files Created/Modified
- `infra/k8s/base/*/serviceaccount.yaml` - Dedicated ServiceAccount per workload with automountServiceAccountToken: false
- `infra/k8s/base/*/deployment.yaml` - Added serviceAccountName and container-level securityContext
- `infra/k8s/base/postgres/statefulset.yaml` - Added serviceAccountName and securityContext (non-root exception)
- `infra/k8s/base/*/kustomization.yaml` - Added serviceaccount.yaml to resources list

## Decisions Made
- postgres uses runAsNonRoot: false because the official postgres image manages privilege dropping internally via gosu
- postgres, keycloak, and rabbitmq use readOnlyRootFilesystem: false due to runtime write requirements (data directories, theme caches, mnesia database)
- securityContext placed at container level (not pod level) for granular per-container control

## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.

## Issues Encountered
None

## User Setup Required
None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- All workloads now have dedicated ServiceAccounts, ready for RBAC policies if needed
- Security contexts established, ready for NetworkPolicy hardening in subsequent phases

# Phase 33 Plan 02: Web Secrets & Keycloak Production Mode Summary

**Web frontend secrets moved to SealedSecret references, Keycloak base switched to production start with dev overlay patch**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 1 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-08T15:13:29Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-08T15:15:10Z
- **Tasks:** 2
- **Files modified:** 6

## Accomplishments
- Removed plain-text AUTH_SECRET and KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET from web deployment manifest
- Web deployment now references web-secrets Secret via secretKeyRef
- Bootstrap script seals and applies web-secrets alongside existing infrastructure secrets
- Keycloak base manifest uses production-mode `start` command
- Dev overlay patches keycloak back to `start-dev` for local development

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Move web secrets to SealedSecret references and update bootstrap** - `9e333e59` (feat)
2. **Task 2: Switch Keycloak base to production mode with dev overlay patch** - `cc1915d5` (feat)

## Files Created/Modified
- `infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml` - AUTH_SECRET and KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET now use secretKeyRef to web-secrets
- `infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh` - Added seal_secret and kubectl apply for web-secrets
- `infra/k8s/base/keycloak/deployment.yaml` - Changed args from start-dev to start
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/keycloak/keycloak-start-dev-patch.yaml` - Strategic merge patch switching to start-dev
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/keycloak/kustomization.yaml` - Dev overlay kustomization referencing base and patch
- `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml` - Changed keycloak reference from base to overlay directory

## Decisions Made
- Web secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) use secretKeyRef to web-secrets SealedSecret, consistent with infra secret pattern
- Keycloak base uses `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev` via strategic merge patch, matching existing overlay patterns for apiservice/gateway/web

## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.

## Issues Encountered
None

## User Setup Required
None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- All secrets now managed via SealedSecrets (both infrastructure and application)
- Keycloak production/dev mode separation ready for environment-specific deployments
- Ready for next phase in v3.1 hardening milestone

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     5→PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
     6→CLUSTER_NAME="micro-commerce"
     7→NAMESPACE="micro-commerce"
     8→SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION="v0.27.3"
     9→ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
    10→
    11→# Color output
    12→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    13→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    14→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    15→
    16→info() { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    17→warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    18→
    19→# --- Step 1: Create kind cluster ---
    20→if kind get clusters 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${CLUSTER_NAME}$"; then
    21→  info "Cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}' already exists, skipping creation."
    22→else
    23→  info "Creating kind cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}'..."
    24→  kind create cluster --name "$CLUSTER_NAME" --config "$SCRIPT_DIR/kind-config.yaml"
    25→fi
    26→
    27→# --- Step 2: Set kubectl context ---
    28→info "Setting kubectl context..."
    29→kubectl cluster-info --context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1
    30→kubectl config use-context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    31→
    32→# --- Step 3: Install SealedSecrets controller ---
    33→info "Installing SealedSecrets controller ${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}..."
    34→kubectl apply -f "https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets/releases/download/${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}/controller.yaml"
    35→
    36→# --- Step 4: Wait for SealedSecrets controller ---
    37→info "Waiting for SealedSecrets controller to be ready..."
    38→kubectl rollout status deployment/sealed-secrets-controller -n kube-system --timeout=120s
    39→
    40→# --- Step 5: Create namespace ---
    41→info "Creating namespace '${NAMESPACE}'..."
    42→kubectl create namespace "$NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    43→
    44→# --- Step 6: Seal dev secrets and apply directly ---
    45→info "Sealing dev secrets..."
    46→
    47→seal_secret() {
    48→  local name="$1"
    49→  local output="$2"
    50→  shift 2
    51→  # Remaining args are --from-literal pairs
    52→
    53→  kubectl create secret generic "$name" \
    54→    --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
    55→    "$@" \
    56→    --dry-run=client -o yaml | \
    57→  kubeseal \
    58→    --controller-name=sealed-secrets-controller \
    59→    --controller-namespace=kube-system \
    60→    --format yaml > "$output"
    61→
    62→  info "  Sealed: $name -> $output"
    63→}
    64→
    65→seal_secret "postgres-credentials" \
    66→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    67→  --from-literal=username=postgres \
    68→  --from-literal=password=postgres
    69→
    70→seal_secret "rabbitmq-credentials" \
    71→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    72→  --from-literal=username=guest \
    73→  --from-literal=password=guest
    74→
    75→seal_secret "keycloak-credentials" \
    76→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    77→  --from-literal=admin-username=admin \
    78→  --from-literal=admin-password=admin
    79→
    80→seal_secret "web-secrets" \
    81→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/web/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    82→  --from-literal=auth-secret=k8s-dev-auth-secret-change-in-production \
    83→  --from-literal=keycloak-client-secret=nextjs-app-secret
    84→
    85→info "Applying sealed secrets..."
    86→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml"
    87→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml"
    88→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml"
    89→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/web/sealed-secret.yaml"
    90→
    91→# --- Step 7: Install ArgoCD ---
    92→info "Installing ArgoCD ${ARGOCD_VERSION}..."
    93→kubectl create namespace argocd --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    94→kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \

    99→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s

  <name>Task 1: Add pre-flight checks, context guard, and trap handler to bootstrap script</name>
  <files>infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh</files>
  <action>
Modify the existing bootstrap.sh to add three safety mechanisms, all inserted BEFORE the "Step 1: Create kind cluster" section:

1. **Required CLI tool check** (immediately after the color/info/warn function definitions):
   - Add a `RED` color variable: `RED='\033[0;31m'`
   - Add an `error()` function: `error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*"; }`
   - Create a `check_prerequisites()` function that:
     - Loops over `kind kubectl kubeseal docker` (docker is also needed for image builds)
     - For each, runs `command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null` and if missing, prints `error "Required tool '$cmd' not found. Please install it before running this script."` and sets a `missing=true` flag
     - After the loop, if `missing=true`, prints a summary message with install hints:
       ```
       error "Install missing tools:"
       error "  kind:      https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/quick-start/#installation"
       error "  kubectl:   https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/"
       error "  kubeseal:  https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets#kubeseal"
       error "  docker:    https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/"
       ```
     - Then `exit 1`
   - Call `check_prerequisites` right after defining it

2. **Trap handler** (after prerequisites check, before Step 1):
   - Define a `cleanup_on_error()` function that prints:
     ```
     echo ""
     error "Bootstrap failed! To clean up:"
     error "  kind delete cluster --name ${CLUSTER_NAME}"
     error "  kubectl config unset contexts.kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
     echo ""
     error "To retry, run this script again."
     ```
   - Set trap: `trap cleanup_on_error ERR`

3. **Context guard** (after the kind cluster creation in Step 1, replacing current Step 2):
   - After cluster creation/skip, get current context: `CURRENT_CONTEXT=$(kubectl config current-context 2>/dev/null || echo "")`
   - Expected context: `EXPECTED_CONTEXT="kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"`
   - If `CURRENT_CONTEXT != EXPECTED_CONTEXT`, print error and exit:
     ```
     error "kubectl context is '${CURRENT_CONTEXT}' but expected '${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}'"
     error "This script only runs against the local kind cluster."
     error "Switch context: kubectl config use-context ${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}"
     exit 1
     ```
   - Keep the existing `kubectl config use-context` call after the guard passes

Do NOT change any other parts of the script (sealed secrets, ArgoCD, image builds, etc.).
  </action>

  </verify>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Fix client-side API URL and convert RabbitMQ to StatefulSet with PVC</name>

  </files>
  <action>
**Client-side API URL fix (REL-01):**

The problem: `/api/config` returns `getApiBaseUrl()` which resolves to `http://gateway:8080` (cluster-internal). The browser cannot reach this. The server-side URL (`services__gateway__*`) is correct for SSR/server components, but client-side needs a browser-reachable URL.

1. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/config.ts`, add a new exported function:
```typescript
export function getClientApiBaseUrl(): string {
  // NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is the browser-reachable gateway URL
  // In K8s: set to http://localhost:38800 (NodePort)
  // In Aspire: the gateway is proxied, so same origin works
  return process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "";
}
```

2. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/api/config/route.ts`, change to:
```typescript
import { getClientApiBaseUrl } from "@/lib/config";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export async function GET() {
  return NextResponse.json({
    apiBaseUrl: getClientApiBaseUrl(),
  });
}
```
When `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is empty string, the client-side `fetch` calls will use relative URLs (same-origin), which works in Aspire where gateway proxies everything. In K8s, the env var is set to `http://localhost:38800`.

3. In `infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml`, add a new env var to the container:
```yaml
- name: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
  value: "http://localhost:38800"
```
Add it after the existing `services__gateway__https__0` env var. The `services__gateway__https__0` continues to be used for server-side data fetching (SSR). `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is used by `/api/config` for client-side browser calls.

**RabbitMQ StatefulSet conversion (REL-02):**

4. Create `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml` — copy the pod template from the existing deployment.yaml but wrap it in a StatefulSet with a volumeClaimTemplate:
   - `kind: StatefulSet`
   - Same metadata labels as current deployment
   - `spec.serviceName: rabbitmq` (must match headless service name)
   - Same replicas (1), selector, template as current deployment
   - Add `volumeClaimTemplates` with a 1Gi PVC named `rabbitmq-data` using `ReadWriteOnce`
   - Add `volumeMounts` to the container: mount `rabbitmq-data` at `/var/lib/rabbitmq`
   - Keep all existing securityContext, env, probes, resources from deployment.yaml

5. Delete `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/deployment.yaml` (replaced by statefulset.yaml)

6. Update `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/kustomization.yaml`: replace `deployment.yaml` with `statefulset.yaml` in resources list

7. Update `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/service.yaml`: the existing ClusterIP service (first document) needs `clusterIP: None` to become a headless service for StatefulSet. Keep the NodePort service (second document) unchanged.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce && grep -q 'NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL' src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/api/config/route.ts src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/config.ts infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml && grep -q 'StatefulSet' infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml && grep -q 'volumeClaimTemplates' infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml && grep -q 'clusterIP: None' infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/service.yaml && test ! -f infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/deployment.yaml && echo "PASS"</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>Client-side /api/config returns browser-reachable URL via NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL. RabbitMQ is a StatefulSet with 1Gi PVC at /var/lib/rabbitmq and headless service. Old deployment.yaml is deleted.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Add startup probes to gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard</name>
  <files>
    infra/k8s/base/gateway/deployment.yaml
    infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml
    infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml
    infra/k8s/base/otel-collector/deployment.yaml
    infra/k8s/base/aspire-dashboard/deployment.yaml
  </files>
  <action>
Add `startupProbe` to each workload. Startup probes tell K8s to wait for slow-starting containers before activating liveness/readiness probes. Use the same endpoint as the readiness probe but with more generous timing (failureThreshold * periodSeconds = max startup time).

For each deployment/statefulset, add a `startupProbe` block in the container spec, BEFORE the existing `livenessProbe`:

1. **gateway** (`infra/k8s/base/gateway/deployment.yaml`):
```yaml
startupProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /health
    port: 8080
  initialDelaySeconds: 5
  periodSeconds: 5
  failureThreshold: 12
```
(allows up to 65s startup)

2. **web** (`infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml`):
```yaml
startupProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /
    port: 3000
  initialDelaySeconds: 5
  periodSeconds: 5
  failureThreshold: 12
```
(allows up to 65s startup)

3. **rabbitmq** (`infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml`):
```yaml
startupProbe:
  exec:
    command: ["rabbitmq-diagnostics", "ping"]
  initialDelaySeconds: 10
  periodSeconds: 5
  failureThreshold: 24
```
(allows up to 130s startup — RabbitMQ with Erlang/mnesia is slow)

4. **otel-collector** (`infra/k8s/base/otel-collector/deployment.yaml`):
```yaml
startupProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /
    port: 13133
  initialDelaySeconds: 3
  periodSeconds: 5
  failureThreshold: 6
```
(allows up to 33s startup)

5. **aspire-dashboard** (`infra/k8s/base/aspire-dashboard/deployment.yaml`):
```yaml
startupProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /
    port: 18888
  initialDelaySeconds: 5
  periodSeconds: 5
  failureThreshold: 12
```
(allows up to 65s startup)

Do NOT modify apiservice or postgres (they already have appropriate probe setups or are not required by REL-03). Do NOT change existing liveness/readiness probes.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce && for f in infra/k8s/base/gateway/deployment.yaml infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml infra/k8s/base/otel-collector/deployment.yaml infra/k8s/base/aspire-dashboard/deployment.yaml; do grep -q 'startupProbe' "$f" || (echo "FAIL: $f missing startupProbe" && exit 1); done && echo "PASS"</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>All 5 workloads (gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, aspire-dashboard) have startup probes with appropriate timeouts for their startup characteristics.</done>
</task>

</tasks>

</verification>

<success_criteria>

- RabbitMQ is a StatefulSet with 1Gi PVC mounted at /var/lib/rabbitmq — data persists across pod restarts

</success_criteria>

# Roadmap: MicroCommerce

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 MVP** — Phases 1-10 (shipped 2026-02-13) — [archive](milestones/v1.0-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v1.1 User Features** — Phases 11-14.3 (shipped 2026-02-14) — [archive](milestones/v1.1-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v2.0 DDD Foundation** — Phases 15-22 (shipped 2026-02-25) — [archive](milestones/v2.0-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps** — Phases 23-29 (shipped 2026-03-03) — [archive](milestones/v3.0-ROADMAP.md)
- 🚧 **v3.1 K8s & GitOps Hardening** — Phases 30-35 (in progress)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 MVP (Phases 1-10) — SHIPPED 2026-02-13</summary>

- [x] Phase 1: Foundation & Project Structure (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-01-30
- [x] Phase 2: Catalog Domain & Admin CRUD (7/7 plans) — completed 2026-01-30
- [x] Phase 3: Catalog Storefront & Seed Data (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-02-07

- [x] Phase 6: Cart Domain (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-09
- [x] Phase 7: Ordering Domain & Checkout (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-10
- [x] Phase 8: Order History & Management (5/5 plans) — completed 2026-02-12
- [x] Phase 9: API Gateway (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-12
- [x] Phase 10: Testing & Polish (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-02-13

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.1 User Features (Phases 11-14.3) — SHIPPED 2026-02-14</summary>

- [x] Phase 11: User Profiles & Authentication Flow (5/5 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 12: Product Reviews & Ratings (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 13: Wishlists & Saved Items (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 14: Integration & Polish (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.1: Check DDD Approach Correctness (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.2: ValueObject Record Struct Migration (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.3: DDD Audit Issue Fixes (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-14

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v2.0 DDD Foundation (Phases 15-22) — SHIPPED 2026-02-25</summary>

- [x] Phase 15: Foundation - Entity Base & Audit Infrastructure (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 16: Conventions - DRY Configuration (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (Phases 23-29) — SHIPPED 2026-03-03</summary>

- [x] Phase 28: Observability (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 29: K8s Client-Side API & Bootstrap Polish (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03

</details>

### v3.1 K8s & GitOps Hardening

**Milestone Goal:** Fix critical security, reliability, and CI/CD issues identified in the v3.0 implementation audit.

- [x] **Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes** - Restore broken workflows with correct SDK, paths, and test gates (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening** - Secure and optimize CI with permissions, caching, and path filters (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene** - Clean up K8s manifests with standard labels, image policies, and overlay structure (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening** - Lock down workloads with security contexts, service accounts, and sealed secrets (completed 2026-03-08)
- [ ] **Phase 34: Reliability Improvements** - Harden runtime with persistent messaging, startup probes, and bootstrap safety
- [ ] **Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices** - Enforce deployment ordering, seal secrets in Git, and scope ArgoCD access

## Phase Details

### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
**Goal**: CI workflows pass on push to master — tests run, images build, releases work
**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase — CI is broken, fix it first)
**Requirements**: CICD-01, CICD-02, CICD-03, CICD-07, CICD-08
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `dotnet-test.yml` workflow passes with .NET 10 SDK and current project paths
  2. `release.yml` builds and publishes images using correct project references
  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing
  4. Dockerfile uses ARG for build-time placeholder secrets (no ENV leaking to runtime)
  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 30-01-PLAN.md — Fix all CI/CD workflows (SDK, paths, test gate, Aspire, Dockerfile secrets)

### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
**Depends on**: Phase 30
**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows

### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads

### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay

### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
**Plans:** 3 plans
Plans:
- [ ] 34-01-PLAN.md — Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler)
- [ ] 34-02-PLAN.md — Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, and startup probes
- [ ] 34-03-PLAN.md — MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts

### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces
**Plans**: TBD

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|-------|--------|-----------|
| 1-10 | v1.0 | 49/49 | Complete | 2026-02-13 |
| 11-14.3 | v1.1 | 23/23 | Complete | 2026-02-14 |
| 15-22 | v2.0 | 9/9 | Complete | 2026-02-25 |
| 23-29 | v3.0 | 29/29 | Complete | 2026-03-03 |
| 30. CI/CD Pipeline Fixes | 1/1 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 31. CI/CD Hardening | 1/1 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 32. Kustomize Hygiene | 2/2 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 33. K8s Security Hardening | 2/2 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 34. Reliability Improvements | v3.1 | 0/3 | Not started | - |
| 35. ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices | v3.1 | 0/TBD | Not started | - |

---
*Roadmap created: 2026-01-29*
*v1.0 shipped: 2026-02-13*
*v1.1 shipped: 2026-02-14*
*v2.0 shipped: 2026-02-25*
*v3.0 shipped: 2026-03-03*

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 34-reliability-improvements
**Plans:** 3 plan(s) in 1 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 34-01, 34-02, 34-03 | yes, yes, yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| 34-01 | Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler) | 1 | infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh |
| 34-02 | Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet with PVC, startup probes | 2 | 9 files (web config, rabbitmq manifests, 5 deployment yamls) |
| 34-03 | MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts | 1 | src/MicroCommerce.ApiService/Program.cs |

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plan |
|-------------|------|
| REL-04 (CLI tool checks) | 34-01 |
| REL-05 (trap handler) | 34-01 |
| REL-06 (context guard) | 34-01 |
| REL-01 (client-side API URL) | 34-02 |
| REL-02 (RabbitMQ StatefulSet) | 34-02 |
| REL-03 (startup probes) | 34-02 |
| REL-07 (outbox on all DbContexts) | 34-03 |

All 7 requirement IDs covered. All 3 plans are Wave 1 (fully parallel, no dependencies between them).

### Key Decisions

- **REL-01**: Introduced `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` env var for browser-reachable gateway URL. Server-side SSR continues using `services__gateway__*`. The `/api/config` route returns the client-side URL.
- **REL-02**: RabbitMQ headless service (`clusterIP: None`) required for StatefulSet. 1Gi PVC at `/var/lib/rabbitmq`.
- **REL-07**: Only 5 of 7 DbContexts need outbox (Catalog, Ordering, Inventory, Reviews, Profiles). Cart and Wishlists do not publish domain events.

### Next Steps

Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase 34-reliability-improvements`

**Phase:** 34-reliability-improvements

| 34-03 | MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts | 1 | src/MicroCommerce.ApiService/Program.cs |

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plan |
|-------------|------|
| REL-04 (CLI tool checks) | 34-01 |
| REL-05 (trap handler) | 34-01 |
| REL-06 (context guard) | 34-01 |

| REL-03 (startup probes) | 34-02 |
| REL-07 (outbox on all DbContexts) | 34-03 |

All 7 requirement IDs covered. All 3 plans are Wave 1 (fully parallel, no dependencies between them).

### Key Decisions

- **REL-01**: Introduced `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` env var for browser-reachable gateway URL. Server-side SSR continues using `services__gateway__*`. The `/api/config` route returns the client-side URL.
- **REL-02**: RabbitMQ headless service (`clusterIP: None`) required for StatefulSet. 1Gi PVC at `/var/lib/rabbitmq`.
- **REL-07**: Only 5 of 7 DbContexts need outbox (Catalog, Ordering, Inventory, Reviews, Profiles). Cart and Wishlists do not publish domain events.

   115→**Client-side API URL fix (REL-01):**
   116→
   117→The problem: `/api/config` returns `getApiBaseUrl()` which resolves to `http://gateway:8080` (cluster-internal). The browser cannot reach this. The server-side URL (`services__gateway__*`) is correct for SSR/server components, but client-side needs a browser-reachable URL.
   118→
   119→1. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/config.ts`, add a new exported function:
   120→```typescript
   121→export function getClientApiBaseUrl(): string {
   122→  // NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is the browser-reachable gateway URL
   123→  // In K8s: set to http://localhost:38800 (NodePort)
   124→  // In Aspire: the gateway is proxied, so same origin works
   125→  return process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "";
   126→}
   127→```
   128→
   129→2. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/api/config/route.ts`, change to:

   135→  return NextResponse.json({
   136→    apiBaseUrl: getClientApiBaseUrl(),
   137→  });
   138→}
   139→```

   141→

   182→Add `startupProbe` to each workload. Startup probes tell K8s to wait for slow-starting containers before activating liveness/readiness probes. Use the same endpoint as the readiness probe but with more generous timing (failureThreshold * periodSeconds = max startup time).
   183→
   184→For each deployment/statefulset, add a `startupProbe` block in the container spec, BEFORE the existing `livenessProbe`:

    84→### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
    85→**Goal**: CI workflows pass on push to master — tests run, images build, releases work
    86→**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase — CI is broken, fix it first)
    87→**Requirements**: CICD-01, CICD-02, CICD-03, CICD-07, CICD-08
    88→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    89→  1. `dotnet-test.yml` workflow passes with .NET 10 SDK and current project paths
    90→  2. `release.yml` builds and publishes images using correct project references
    91→  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing
    92→  4. Dockerfile uses ARG for build-time placeholder secrets (no ENV leaking to runtime)
    93→  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
    94→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
    95→Plans:
    96→- [ ] 30-01-PLAN.md — Fix all CI/CD workflows (SDK, paths, test gate, Aspire, Dockerfile secrets)
    97→
    98→### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
    99→**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 30
   101→**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
   104→  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
   105→  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
   106→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
   107→Plans:
   108→- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows
   109→
   110→### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
   111→**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
   112→**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
   113→**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
   114→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   115→  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
   116→  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
   117→  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
   118→  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
   119→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   120→Plans:
   121→- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
   122→- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads
   123→
   124→### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
   125→**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
   126→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
   127→**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04
   128→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   129→  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
   130→  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
   131→  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
   132→  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
   133→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   134→Plans:
   135→- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
   136→- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay
   137→
   138→### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
   139→**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
   140→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
   141→**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
   142→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   143→  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
   144→  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
   145→  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
   146→  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
   147→  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
   148→  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
   149→  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
   150→**Plans:** 3 plans
   151→Plans:
   152→- [ ] 34-01-PLAN.md — Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler)
   153→- [ ] 34-02-PLAN.md — Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, and startup probes
   154→- [ ] 34-03-PLAN.md — MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts
   155→
   156→### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
   157→**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
   158→**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
   159→**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
   160→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   161→  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
   162→  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
   163→  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
   164→  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
   165→  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
   166→  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces

content.text:
Found 3 plans in `.planning/phases/34-reliability-improvements` (3 incomplete)

## Execution Plan

**Phase 34: Reliability Improvements** — 3 plans across 1 wave

| Wave | Plans | What it builds |
|------|-------|----------------|
| 1 | 34-01, 34-02, 34-03 | Bootstrap safety, RabbitMQ PVC + probes + API URL, MassTransit outbox |

---
## Wave 1

**34-01: Bootstrap Script Safety**
Adds pre-flight CLI tool checks (kind/kubectl/kubeseal), kubectl context guard for the expected kind cluster, and a trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure.

**34-02: K8s Reliability (API URL, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, Startup Probes)**
Fixes client-side API URL resolution so browser calls reach the gateway via localhost. Converts RabbitMQ from Deployment to StatefulSet with PVC for message persistence. Adds startup probes to gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard.

**34-03: MassTransit Outbox Registration**
Registers MassTransit EF Core outbox on all DbContexts that publish domain events (Ordering, Inventory, Reviews, Profiles) — currently only CatalogDbContext has it.

Spawning 3 agents in parallel...

    32→<objective>
    33→Add pre-flight safety checks and error handling to the bootstrap script.
    34→
    35→Purpose: Prevent confusing failures when prerequisites are missing or when the script accidentally targets the wrong cluster.
    36→Output: Updated bootstrap.sh with CLI tool checks, context guard, and trap-based cleanup guidance.
    37→</objective>
    38→
    39→<execution_context>
    40→@/Users/baotoq/.claude/get-shit-done/workflows/execute-plan.md

    50→
    51→<tasks>
    52→
    53→<task type="auto">
    54→  <name>Task 1: Add pre-flight checks, context guard, and trap handler to bootstrap script</name>
    55→  <files>infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh</files>
    56→  <action>
    57→Modify the existing bootstrap.sh to add three safety mechanisms, all inserted BEFORE the "Step 1: Create kind cluster" section:
    58→
    59→1. **Required CLI tool check** (immediately after the color/info/warn function definitions):
    60→   - Add a `RED` color variable: `RED='\033[0;31m'`
    61→   - Add an `error()` function: `error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*"; }`
    62→   - Create a `check_prerequisites()` function that:
    63→     - Loops over `kind kubectl kubeseal docker` (docker is also needed for image builds)
    64→     - For each, runs `command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null` and if missing, prints `error "Required tool '$cmd' not found. Please install it before running this script."` and sets a `missing=true` flag
    65→     - After the loop, if `missing=true`, prints a summary message with install hints:
    66→       ```
    67→       error "Install missing tools:"
    68→       error "  kind:      https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/quick-start/#installation"
    69→       error "  kubectl:   https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/"
    70→       error "  kubeseal:  https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets#kubeseal"
    71→       error "  docker:    https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/"
    72→       ```
    73→     - Then `exit 1`
    74→   - Call `check_prerequisites` right after defining it
    75→
    76→2. **Trap handler** (after prerequisites check, before Step 1):
    77→   - Define a `cleanup_on_error()` function that prints:
    78→     ```
    79→     echo ""
    80→     error "Bootstrap failed! To clean up:"
    81→     error "  kind delete cluster --name ${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    82→     error "  kubectl config unset contexts.kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    83→     echo ""
    84→     error "To retry, run this script again."
    85→     ```
    86→   - Set trap: `trap cleanup_on_error ERR`
    87→
    88→3. **Context guard** (after the kind cluster creation in Step 1, replacing current Step 2):
    89→   - After cluster creation/skip, get current context: `CURRENT_CONTEXT=$(kubectl config current-context 2>/dev/null || echo "")`
    90→   - Expected context: `EXPECTED_CONTEXT="kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"`
    91→   - If `CURRENT_CONTEXT != EXPECTED_CONTEXT`, print error and exit:
    92→     ```
    93→     error "kubectl context is '${CURRENT_CONTEXT}' but expected '${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}'"
    94→     error "This script only runs against the local kind cluster."
    95→     error "Switch context: kubectl config use-context ${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}"
    96→     exit 1
    97→     ```
    98→   - Keep the existing `kubectl config use-context` call after the guard passes
    99→
   100→Do NOT change any other parts of the script (sealed secrets, ArgoCD, image builds, etc.).
   101→  </action>
   102→  <verify>
   103→    <automated>bash -n infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'command -v' infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'trap cleanup_on_error' infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh && grep -q 'EXPECTED_CONTEXT' infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh && echo "PASS"</automated>
   104→  </verify>
   105→  <done>Bootstrap script has: (1) pre-flight check for kind/kubectl/kubeseal/docker, (2) trap handler printing cleanup guidance on ERR, (3) context guard refusing to run if not on kind-micro-commerce context. Script passes bash syntax check.</done>
   106→</task>
   107→
   108→</tasks>
   109→

   112→- Script contains `command -v kind`, `command -v kubectl`, `command -v kubeseal`
   113→- Script contains `trap cleanup_on_error ERR`
   114→- Script contains context comparison with `kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}`
   115→</verification>
   116→

   119→- On any command failure, trap handler prints cleanup commands (kind delete cluster, unset context)
   120→- Script refuses to proceed if kubectl context is not kind-micro-commerce
   121→</success_criteria>

   114→  <action>
   115→**Client-side API URL fix (REL-01):**
   116→
   117→The problem: `/api/config` returns `getApiBaseUrl()` which resolves to `http://gateway:8080` (cluster-internal). The browser cannot reach this. The server-side URL (`services__gateway__*`) is correct for SSR/server components, but client-side needs a browser-reachable URL.
   118→
   119→1. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/config.ts`, add a new exported function:
   120→```typescript
   121→export function getClientApiBaseUrl(): string {
   122→  // NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is the browser-reachable gateway URL
   123→  // In K8s: set to http://localhost:38800 (NodePort)
   124→  // In Aspire: the gateway is proxied, so same origin works
   125→  return process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "";
   126→}
   127→```
   128→
   129→2. In `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/api/config/route.ts`, change to:
   130→```typescript
   131→import { getClientApiBaseUrl } from "@/lib/config";
   132→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
   133→
   134→export async function GET() {
   135→  return NextResponse.json({
   136→    apiBaseUrl: getClientApiBaseUrl(),
   137→  });
   138→}
   139→```
   140→When `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is empty string, the client-side `fetch` calls will use relative URLs (same-origin), which works in Aspire where gateway proxies everything. In K8s, the env var is set to `http://localhost:38800`.
   141→
   142→3. In `infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml`, add a new env var to the container:
   143→```yaml
   144→- name: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
   145→  value: "http://localhost:38800"
   146→```
   147→Add it after the existing `services__gateway__https__0` env var. The `services__gateway__https__0` continues to be used for server-side data fetching (SSR). `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is used by `/api/config` for client-side browser calls.
   148→
   149→**RabbitMQ StatefulSet conversion (REL-02):**
   150→
   151→4. Create `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/statefulset.yaml` — copy the pod template from the existing deployment.yaml but wrap it in a StatefulSet with a volumeClaimTemplate:
   152→   - `kind: StatefulSet`
   153→   - Same metadata labels as current deployment
   154→   - `spec.serviceName: rabbitmq` (must match headless service name)
   155→   - Same replicas (1), selector, template as current deployment
   156→   - Add `volumeClaimTemplates` with a 1Gi PVC named `rabbitmq-data` using `ReadWriteOnce`
   157→   - Add `volumeMounts` to the container: mount `rabbitmq-data` at `/var/lib/rabbitmq`
   158→   - Keep all existing securityContext, env, probes, resources from deployment.yaml
   159→
   160→5. Delete `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/deployment.yaml` (replaced by statefulset.yaml)
   161→
   162→6. Update `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/kustomization.yaml`: replace `deployment.yaml` with `statefulset.yaml` in resources list
   163→
   164→7. Update `infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/service.yaml`: the existing ClusterIP service (first document) needs `clusterIP: None` to become a headless service for StatefulSet. Keep the NodePort service (second document) unchanged.
   165→  </action>

   168→  </verify>
   169→  <done>Client-side /api/config returns browser-reachable URL via NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL. RabbitMQ is a StatefulSet with 1Gi PVC at /var/lib/rabbitmq and headless service. Old deployment.yaml is deleted.</done>
   170→</task>

   173→  <name>Task 2: Add startup probes to gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard</name>
   174→  <files>

   176→    infra/k8s/base/web/deployment.yaml
